questions.q: What does the abbreviation DRDO stand for?
options: Directorate of Research in Defence Ordnance
questions.explanation: DRDO stands for the Defence Research and Development Organisation, India's premier military R&D agency.

questions.q: In which year was DRDO established?
questions.explanation: DRDO was formed in 1958 by merging earlier defence research bodies into a single organisation.

questions.q: Under which Indian ministry does DRDO function?
questions.explanation: DRDO works under the Department of Defence Research and Development within the Ministry of Defence.

questions.q: Which famous family of long-range ballistic missiles was developed by DRDO?
questions.explanation: The Agni series of long-range ballistic missiles is one of DRDO's flagship strategic programmes.

questions.q: Which DRDO-developed missile is a supersonic cruise missile made jointly with Russia?
questions.explanation: BrahMos is a supersonic cruise missile developed by BrahMos Aerospace, a DRDO joint venture with Russia's NPO Mashinostroyeniya.

questions.q: DRDO's headquarters are located in which Indian city?
questions.explanation: DRDO is headquartered at DRDO Bhawan in New Delhi.

questions.q: Which former Indian President was a key aerospace scientist associated with DRDO's missile programme?
questions.explanation: A. P. J. Abdul Kalam, the 'Missile Man of India', led DRDO's Integrated Guided Missile Development Programme.

questions.q: The indigenous Light Combat Aircraft associated with DRDO's design work is named?
questions.explanation: Tejas is India's indigenous Light Combat Aircraft, developed with DRDO's Aeronautical Development Agency.

questions.q: What kind of systems is DRDO best known for developing?
questions.explanation: DRDO develops missiles, radars, aircraft, armaments and other strategic defence systems for India's armed forces.

questions.q: DRDO operates a network of what kind of facilities across India?
questions.explanation: DRDO runs a large network of specialised defence research laboratories spread across the country.

questions.q: Which integrated programme launched in 1983 produced missiles like Prithvi, Agni, Akash, Trishul and Nag?
questions.explanation: The Integrated Guided Missile Development Programme (IGMDP), launched in 1983 under A. P. J. Abdul Kalam, produced Prithvi, Agni, Akash, Trishul and Nag.

questions.q: Which DRDO missile under the IGMDP is a short-range surface-to-surface ballistic missile?
questions.explanation: Prithvi is DRDO's short-range surface-to-surface ballistic missile developed under the IGMDP.

questions.q: Akash, developed by DRDO, is what type of missile system?
questions.explanation: Akash is a medium-range surface-to-air missile system developed by DRDO to defend against aerial threats.

questions.q: The DRDO missile 'Nag' is designed primarily for which role?
questions.explanation: Nag is a third-generation, fire-and-forget anti-tank guided missile developed by DRDO.

questions.q: Which DRDO laboratory in Hyderabad is the lead facility for missile development?
questions.explanation: The Defence Research and Development Laboratory (DRDL) in Hyderabad is DRDO's principal missile-development lab.

questions.q: Astra, developed by DRDO, is what kind of missile?
questions.explanation: Astra is India's first indigenous beyond-visual-range air-to-air missile, developed by DRDO.

questions.q: In March 2019 DRDO demonstrated India's anti-satellite capability in a test code-named?
questions.explanation: Mission Shakti was DRDO's March 2019 anti-satellite (ASAT) test that destroyed a satellite in low Earth orbit.

questions.q: Which DRDO laboratory specialises in research on high-altitude and extreme-environment physiology and food?
options: Defence Institute of Physiology and Allied Sciences
questions.explanation: The Defence Institute of Physiology and Allied Sciences (DIPAS) studies human performance in extreme environments such as high altitude.

questions.q: Which DRDO programme developed the indigenous airborne early warning and control system known as 'Netra'?
questions.explanation: DRDO's Airborne Early Warning and Control (AEW&C) programme produced the 'Netra' system mounted on Embraer aircraft.

questions.q: The indigenous gas turbine engine project that DRDO developed for fighter aircraft is named?
questions.explanation: The Kaveri engine is DRDO's indigenous gas turbine engine project, originally intended for the Tejas fighter.

questions.q: DRDO was formed in 1958 by combining the Defence Science Organisation with what?
options: The existing technical development establishments of the armed services
The Council of Scientific and Industrial Research
questions.explanation: DRDO was created in 1958 by amalgamating the Defence Science Organisation with the technical development establishments and directorates of the armed services.

questions.q: The senior official who heads DRDO also serves in which advisory capacity to the government?
options: Scientific Adviser to the Raksha Mantri (Defence Minister)
questions.explanation: The DRDO Chairman traditionally also serves as the Scientific Adviser to the Defence Minister (Raksha Mantri).

questions.q: DRDO's first major missile project, begun in the early 1970s to develop a surface-to-air missile, was code-named?
questions.explanation: Project Devil was DRDO's early effort to reverse-engineer a surface-to-air missile, providing experience that later fed into the IGMDP.

questions.q: DRDO's early ballistic-missile technology effort that ran alongside Project Devil was code-named?
questions.explanation: Project Valiant was DRDO's early programme aimed at developing long-range ballistic-missile technology.

questions.q: The first missile of the Agni series, Agni-I, was first test-fired (as a technology demonstrator) in which year?
questions.explanation: The Agni technology demonstrator was first successfully test-fired in 1989, validating India's re-entry and propulsion technology.

questions.q: Which DRDO laboratory developed the metallurgical and materials technology, including special alloys, for India's strategic systems?
options: Centre for Fire, Explosive and Environment Safety
questions.explanation: The Defence Metallurgical Research Laboratory (DMRL) in Hyderabad develops advanced alloys and materials for India's defence systems.

questions.q: Agni-V, DRDO's longest-range ballistic missile, is generally classified as which type?
questions.explanation: Agni-V is DRDO's longest-range missile, with a reach of over 5,000 km placing it in the intercontinental/intermediate-range class.

questions.q: Which DRDO-developed subsonic cruise missile is designed for long-range, terrain-hugging strikes?
questions.explanation: Nirbhay is DRDO's long-range, subsonic cruise missile capable of low-altitude terrain-following flight.

questions.q: DRDO's Electronics and Radar Development Establishment (LRDE) is located in which city?
questions.explanation: The Electronics and Radar Development Establishment (LRDE), DRDO's lead radar lab, is based in Bengaluru.

questions.q: DRDO has developed a two-tiered Ballistic Missile Defence system using which pair of interceptors?
options: Prithvi Air Defence (PAD) and Advanced Air Defence (AAD)
questions.explanation: DRDO's Ballistic Missile Defence programme uses the Prithvi Air Defence (PAD) for exo-atmospheric and the Advanced Air Defence (AAD) for endo-atmospheric interception.
